sglang.title: Multi-turn benchmark for hierarchical caching
sglang.author: xiezhq-hermann
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This is one of the breakdown PR of the hierarchical caching proposal.

Modifications
This microbenchmark simulates multiple concurrent clients interacting with a server. Requests are sent according to defined distributions, and KV caches of historical contexts gradually accumulate until G

sglang.title: Query remaining memory dynamically for PrefillAdder
sglang.author: xiezhq-hermann
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This is one of the breakdown PR of the hierarchical caching proposal.
Modifications
Dynamically query the remaining memory quota to accommodate overlapped I/O operations. This approach resolves the issues of the previous static quota calculation, which often underestimated or overestimate

vllm.title: [core] allow callable in collective_rpc
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Users find it annoying if they just want to execute a simple function but they need to inherit the worker class. This PR makes it possible to directly pass a function (callable).

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix score api for missing max_model_len validation
vllm.author: wallashss
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Serving score is not properly validating input and it makes the engine crash when the input is longer than the max model len. Following the recipe to repro:
 python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model BAAI/bge-reranker-large --max-model-len 5

curl http://localhost:8000/score -H "Content-T

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Mistral tokenizer encode accept list of str
vllm.author: jikunshang

vllm.title: [AMD][FP8] Using MI300 FP8 format on ROCm for block_quant
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Requantizing fp8 weights into NANOO format on ROCm platform.
Conditionally using e4m3fnuz where appropriate.
This is essential for DeepSeek V3 support

vllm.title: [torch.compile] disable logging when cache is disabled
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
disable the logging of Cache the graph of shape None for later use
test command: VLLM_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 vllm serve meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B -O3

vllm.title: [misc] fix cross-node TP
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: In #11256 , the code check in multiprocessing executor is incorrectly moved to platforms , and therefore applies to ray. this breaks the multi-node TP support, as reported in #12132 , and also causes problems like https://buildkite.com/vllm/ci/builds/12025#01946b8d-cc0b-4af1-bb73-5093f0c45f8d/145-43

vllm.title: [AMD][CI/Build][Bugfix] updated pytorch stale wheel path by using stable wheel
vllm.author: hongxiayang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #12066
This PR is to replace the stale nightly wheel path for ROCm 6.2 pytorch with the stable pytorch wheel.
When the Dockerfile.rocm was previously upgraded to ROCm 6.2, the stable wheel does not exist yet for ROCm 6.2 in pytorch,  therefore a nightly wheel path was used.
Since the current sta

vllm.title: [core] further polish memory profiling
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Improve upon #11809
current main branch, vllm serve meta-llama/Llama-3.2-11B-Vision --load-format dummy --max-model-len 65536 --max-num-seqs 128 --enforce-eager will fail.
after this PR, it can work on H100-80G now:
INFO 01-16 09:40:00 worker.py:238] the current vLLM instance can use total_gpu_memor

vllm.title: [Model] Port deepseek-vl2 processor and remove `deepseek_vl2` dependency
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-VL2 is not compatible with transformers >= 4.48.0, which may cause deepseek-vl2 model broken after we updating transformers to 4.48.0 in #12120
Porting the deepseek-vl2's processor to make the model compatible with transformers==4.48.0

vllm.title: [core] clean up executor class hierarchy between v1 and v0
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a followup work of #11256 . After that PR, the executor hierarchy becomes quite messy:

v1 and v0 share the same ray executor
v1's multiprocessing executor and uniproc executor does not inherit any class from v0's executor base class

This makes adding new functions quite confusing. For exam
